text:
It shouldn芒聙聶t come as a surprise who was the firstFlorida Gatorto win gold at the2024 Paris Olympics.脗

As part of the 4x100-meter freestyle relay for the United States, former Gators swimmer Caeleb Dressel (2015-2018) helped win Team USA芒聙聶s first gold medal. It芒聙聶s his eighth Olympic gold medal overall, which comprises every medal he芒聙聶s won. It was also his third goal medal in this event.

TEAM USA STRIKES GOLD. 冒聼聮陋CAELEB DRESSEL BRINGS IT HOME IN THE MEN'S 4X100 FREE RELAY.#ParisOlympicspic.twitter.com/rJwrSEWhps

The anchor of the group, Dressel notched a 47.53-second 100-meter individual swim to seal the top spot. As a group, the United States芒聙聶 medley team finished with an overall time of 3:09.28, beating out rivals Australia (silver) and Italy (bronze).

Next, Dressel will compete in the 50-meter freestyle on Aug. 2. He芒聙聶ll also compete in the 100-meter butterfly the following day and the 4x100-meter medley relay on Aug. 4. Dressel won gold medals in all of those events in Tokyo in 2021.脗

Dressel wasn芒聙聶t the only Gator to be a finalist in the event. Josh Liendo (2022-present) anchored Canada to a sixth-place finish.脗

Other Gators of Note

Two more UF swimmers began Olympic play on Saturday.脗

Venezuela芒聙聶s Alfonso Mestre (2019-2023) and the United States芒聙聶 Kieran Smith (2018-2022) each finished sixth in their respective heats for the 400-meter freestyle and did not advance to the finals. Smith芒聙聶s participation in the Paris games is complete. Mestre has one more event lined up in the 800-meter freestyle.

On the hardwood, a pair of former Gators matched up to begin group play.脗

Canada芒聙聶s Andrew Nembhard (2018-2020) and Greece芒聙聶sNick Calathes(2007-2009) made their Olympic debuts in Group A, with Canada winning 86-79. Calathes finished with 3 points on 1-of-4 shooting while Nembhard finished with 2 points on 1-of-1 shooting.脗

On Tuesday, Calathes and Greece will next play Spain at 5 p.m. ET, while Nembhard and Canada will play Australia at 7:30 p.m. ET.脗

Finally, former UF tennis player Danielle Collins (2012-2013) of the United States was set to begin singles play on Saturday, but had her match scratched due to poor weather conditions on the outdoor courts. She will play Germany芒聙聶s Laura Siegemond on Sunday.

Cam Parker is a reporter covering the Florida Gators, Auburn Tigers and Clemson Tigers with a degree in journalism from the University of Florida. He also covers and broadcasts Alachua County high school sports with The Prep Zone and Mainstreet Daily News. When he isn't writing, he enjoys listening to '70s music such as The Band or Lynyrd Skynyrd, binge-watching shows and playing with his cat, Chester, and dog, Rufus.

title: Ex-Gators Swimmer Caeleb Dressel Off to Fast Start at Olympics Games in Paris
description: Former Florida Gators swimmer Caeleb Dressel helped Team USA win its first gold medal of the 2024 Summer Olympics.
